Identify hazardous materials
Safely load and transport them
properly placard your vehicle

who is responsible for refusing improper shipments and reporting accidents involving hazardous materials?
who refuses leaking packages, placards vehicle while being loaded and safely transports the shipment without delay?
What is class 3?
Flammable gases, non flammable gases and poisonous/toxic gases all belong to class...
After an accident you may be unable to communicate, this is why having accurate ____________ in the correct spot is very important.
T or F the pouch of the drivers door, in clear reachable view from drivers seat, and on the drivers seat (when away from vehicle) are appropriate placed to keep shipping papers
_________ are diamond shaped hazard warnings which need to be attached to the hazardous material in some way.
A placarded vehicle must have at least how many identical placards displayed

qas.q: FALSE all four sides are required to have one
qas.a: T or F the front of the truck/tractor does not need a placard
The ____________ displays both the name of class/division of the material and the division number for other drivers to see.
Column 1: Symbol that means the material is subject to hazardous material regulations only when it is being transported by air
Column 1: Symbol that means the material is subject to hazardous material regulations only when it is being transported by water
Column 1: Symbol meaning proper shipping name works for within the US but maybe not for international
Column 1: symbol means shipping name can be used in international transportation (name may be different within US)
Column 1: symbol meaning the hazardous material is identified by a generic shipping name
Column 2: proper shipping names are shown in normal fonts, names shown in _________ are not proper shipping names
If column 3 in the hazardous materials table says forbidden, should you transport it?

qas.q: The number is the same, UN can travel internationally and NA (north american) can only travel between the US and Canada
qas.a: what is the difference between an identification number (column 4)with UN and NA?
If the word "inhalation hazard appear on the shipping paper/package you must display what type of placards?

page numbers
proper shipping description for each hazardous material
a shipper's certification signed by the shipper

entered first
highlighted

ID number

total quantity and unit of measure
number and types of packages
the letters RQ if its a reportable quantity
if RQ the name of the hazardous substance
for all materials with the letter G in column 1, the tech name

name and address of shipper
hazardous material's ID number and shipping name
the labels required

packages with ________ containers inside will have package orientation markings with arrows pointing in the correct upright position

qas.q: close together and near the proper shipping name

3
contrasting
prohibited
tractor or the trailer

qas.a: placards must be easily seen from all directions, read from left to right, atleast ___ inches away from other markings, kept clear of attachments or devices like ladders & doors, be fixed to a background of a ______ color, the use of "drive safely" and other slogans is ___________, front placard may be on the front of the _________ or the _________, the placard must be undamaged and __________.

qas.q:
1. hazard class of the material
2. amount of materials shipped
3. total weight of all classes of hazardous material on the vehicle

qas.q: any amount of substance being transported
qas.a: Placard table one includes materials which must be placarded when?
Placard table two materials must be placarded when?

qas.q:
you have 2 or more table 2 placards needed
you have not loaded 2205 lbs of any table 2 hazmat at any one place
qas.a: "DANGEROUS" placards can replace the seperate table 2 placards when...
T or F: the dangerous placard is optional and can legally be replaced by individual material placards
T or F: if the words inhalation hazard appear on the shpping paper/package, you must display "poison gas"/"poison inhalation" placards in addition to any other placards needed
materials with a secondary hazard of being dangerous when wet must display what placard in addition to all other necessary placards?

qas.q: FALSE: as long as the placard is accurate, you can display it
qas.a: T or F: you may not display a hazmat placard if it is not required for the material being transported

qas.q: mass explosives, project hazards, mass fire hazards, poisonous/toxic gases, dangerous when wet, poison/toxic inhalation, radioactive

qas.q: minor explosion, insensitive explosives, flammable/nonflammable gases, flammable/nonflammable liquids, combustible liquids, flammable solids, spontaneously combustible, oxidizers, infectious substances, corrosives, miscellaneous hazardous materials
qas.a: name placard table 2 substances
before loading or unloading what is the first thing you should set to keep the truck from moving?
You should always load hazmat away from sources of _______.
when loading/unloading watch for signs of ________ from the package. if you find these do not transport them as they are usually dangerous

qas.q:
1. Explosives
2. Flammable Gas
3. Flammable Liquid
4. Flammable Solids
5. Oxidizers
qas.a: Do not let people smoke nearby what classes
All hazardous materials must be _________ during transportation in order to keep them from falling, sliding, bouncing, etc.
Cargo heaters are forbidden when transporting classes...
You cannot have overhang or tailgate loads of classes... Unlelss the packages are fire/water resistant or covered with a fire/water resistant tarp
Your engine should always be ______ when loading or unloading explosives
Class __ and __ materials may react when in contact with water, heat, or air. For this reason they must be kept dry and completely enclosed/covered securely

Class 8 (Corrosive) Materials must always be kept upright and if fragile loaded one by one. they must be loaded onto an even floor surface and ________ acid can not be stacked above any other product.
compressed gases must be loaded so that they are ___________ ______________ or in racks/boxes attached to the vehicle.
Poisonous gas/materials should never be transported in containers with _________. Never load these packeages in the cab or sleeper.
T or F: some radioactive materials can have radiation surrounding the package and passing into nearby packages
cargo tanks are ________ packaging permanently attached to a vehicle
_________________ tanks are bulk packaging not permanently attached to a vehicle.
portable tanks must show the owners name, the name of contents on 2 sides in letters atleast 2 inches tall if contents are more than _________ gallons and 1 inch if less.
the person watching the tank loading must be alert, have a clear view, be within ______ feet of the tank, know the hazards involved, know emergency procedures, and be authorized to move the tank
the only reason to run your engine when loading a flammable liquid is if it is _____________ __ __________. Otherwise it should be off.
Liquid discharge valves on a compressed gas tank should be closed unless you are__________ or ____________.

qas.q:
bridge, tunnel, building
place where people gather
open fire
qas.a: If transporting explosives you can't park withing 300 feet of ...
a ______________ ____________ is an approved place where you can park an unattended vehicle containing explosives
when is it allowed to use flares to signal for help if a breakdown occurs?
some states/counties have route restrictions on hazmat items. drivers should __________ try to avoid places with alot of people around
If transporting ____________________ you must have a written route plan and follow it. It must be with you in the truck
Do not smoke within ___ feet of a vehicle containing flammable gases, explosives, etc.
Drivers transporting chlorine must have an ________ ______ _______________ with them in the vehicle.
stop ___ to ___ feet before a railroad if your vehicle is placarded, carries any amount of chlorine, or has cargo tanks used for haz mat (loaded or empty)
USDOT puts together this document so first responders know how to react to accidents with different hazmat materials.

qas.q:
keep people away
limit the spread of material IF IT IS SAFE
communciate the danger of the materials to responders
provide shipping papers/emergency response info to them
qas.a: As a professional driver, your job at the scene of a crash is to....
T or F unless specially trained you should not attempt to put out a hazardous material fire. (Minor truck fires are fine to put out)

qas.q: FALSE: the air getting to the flames will make the fire accelerate
qas.a: T or F if the doors of your cargo trailer are hot during a fire you should open them to let the heat out

If there is a leak, first you need to ________ the material. Do not touch it, it could injure you. Do not try to identify it by smell. Only move your vehicle if it is to get it off the road. Never continue driving.

qas.q:
description of the emergency
exact location and direction of travel
your name, carrier's name, name of city of terminal
proper shipping name, hazard class, and ID # of material
qas.a: If you need to send someone for a phone/truck stop to communicate an emergency, make sure they know... usually it is best to write it down.
how far should you park from the travelled part of a roadway?
